memoryArtifacts.headline: William de Beauchamp III, who was born at Elmley Castle in Warwickshire in 1215 and is burried at St. Mary’s Church there with his wife Isab
memoryArtifacts.description: William de Beauchamp III, who was born at Elmley Castle in Warwickshire in 1215 and is burried at St. Mary’s Church there with his wife Isabel Mauduit Beauchamp.

memoryArtifacts.headline: Only earthworks remain of the late 11th century Elmely Castle, along with traces of a stone keep and curtain wall added 100 years later

zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Beauchamp於大約1215年出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾時，他的父親Walter de Beauchamp24歲，母親Joan de Mortimer26歲。他於大約1235年與Isabel Mauduit在英國武斯特斯爾艾爾姆利 卡素結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他於1237年被列為sheriff of worcestershire。他去世於1268年1月7日享年54歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾伍斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Beauchamp于大约1215年出生在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾时，他的父亲Walter de Beauchamp24岁，母亲Joan de Mortimer26岁。他于大约1235年与Isabel Mauduit在英国武斯特斯爾艾爾姆利 卡素结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他于1237年被列为sheriff of worcestershire。他去世于1268年1月7日享年54岁，埋葬在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾伍斯特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Beauchamp was born about 1215, in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Walter de Beauchamp, was 24 and his mother, Joan de Mortimer, was 26. He married Isabel Mauduit about 1235, in Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 7 daughters. In 1237, his occupation is listed as sheriff of worcestershire. He died on 7 January 1268, at the age of 54, and was buried in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Beauchamp est né environ 1215, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Walter de Beauchamp, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Joan de Mortimer, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Isabel Mauduit environ 1235, à Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 7 filles. En 1237, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of worcestershire. Il est décédé le 7 janvier 1268, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Worcester, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat um 1235 in Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England Isabel Mauduit geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 7 Töchter. 1237 ist sein Beruf als sheriff of worcestershire aufgeführt. Er starb am 7. Januar 1268 im Alter von 54 Jahren und wurde in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Isabel Mauduit circa 1235, nel Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 7 figlie. Nel 1237, la sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of worcestershire. ja.lifeSummary: William de Beauchampが約1215年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアで生まれた時，父Walter de Beauchampは24歳，母Joan de Mortimerは26歳であった。約1235年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアエルムリー カストルでIsabel Mauduitと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。1237年，職業はsheriff of worcestershireと記録されている。彼は1268年1月7日に54歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアウオルセスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1215년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter de Beauchamp(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Mortimer(은)는 26세였다. 그는 대략 1235년 영국 워스스터실르 엘리 카스틀에서 Isabel Mauduit(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 1237년, 그의 직업은 sheriff of worcestershire이었다. 그는 1268년 1월 7일 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 워스스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1215, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Walter de Beauchamp, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Mortimer, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabel Mauduit aproximadamente 1235, em Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 7 filhas. Em 1237, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of worcestershire. Ele faleceu em 7 de janeiro de 1268, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Beauchamp родился около 1215 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Walter de Beauchamp было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Joan de Mortimer, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Mauduit около 1235 г. в местности элмли кастл, ворсестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 7 дочерей. В 1237 году он работал по профессии sheriff of worcestershire. Он умер 7 января 1268 г. в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William de Beauchamp nació aproximadamente 1215, en Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Walter de Beauchamp, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Joan de Mortimer, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Isabel Mauduit aproximadamente 1235, en Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 7 hijas. En 1237, su ocupación registrada era sheriff of worcestershire. Murió el 7 de enero de 1268, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
